page.title: Quarks – Quantum Martial Arts San Francisco
page.meta_description: Quarks classes at Quantum Martial Arts offer movement play and motor skills development for ages 4 through 5 years old.

content.visible_text: Quarks Martial arts, motor skills, and movement play, for ages 4 – 5. The Quarks program at Quantum is uniquely designed for our youngest karatekas. Classes begin with an obstacle course, include stretching and balance exercises, then target practice and martial arts line drills. All classes end with circle time where the class talks about the tenets of the martial arts: courtesy, integrity, perseverance, self-control, and indomitable spirit. Schedule All Quarks classes will be held in-person only. We will follow all COVID precautions required by the most current guidance from the San Francisco Dept. of Health regarding youth programming found here . Class will take place: Monday, Wednesdays, 4:15–5:00 p.m. and Fridays 4:00-4:45pm A minimum of 2x/wk attendance is required, and all students start with a mandatory orientation meeting with Master Evans, where one guardian must be present. Sign up for Quarks classes here! Class Description Movement play games and martial arts exercises tailored to the unique needs of pre-K students are designed to develop and strengthen the neural connection between mind and body as kids discover where their body is, how to stay safe and recover quickly during playtime, learn right from left, and translate spoken directions into actions in their body. They will also learn soft social skills such as sharing, taking turns, following rules, telling the truth, controlling emotions, taking chances, and trying new things, perseverance, and bravery. Classes are 45 minutes long, and kids will be engaged in movement play, games, songs, motor skills exercises, and of course martial arts techniques that cultivate body-awareness, balance, and coordination during the crucial early developmental years. The Quantum Dojo has a large, open floor, with a soft cushioned white mat, and is filled with natural light. Martial arts classes provide excellent opportunities for kids to learn about boundaries and how to communicate needs to their peers and teachers. Martial arts can be great for shy, quiet, or reserved kids, giving them an opportunity to find their voice and express themselves with power through their bodies. Martial arts can also be a successful tool for kids who are showing signs of aggression or having trouble controlling their emotions through outbursts, tantrums, hitting or attacking parents, siblings, or kids at school, as the structure of the Dojo facilitates creating clear boundaries, and gives kids a safe place to physically express emotions by punching and kicking heavy bags and targets. Tuition As with all Quantum classes, the first week of trial classes is FREE! Quantum is a registered CA 501(c)(3) nonprofit, and our price structure is designed to make classes available to all. As such, we offer sliding scale to every students who needs it. Suggested monthly dues: $175 per child. These dues include access to unlimited classes. Yearly membership fee: $60 per student. Uniforms: $30–$50 Enroll Today! Click here to sign up for a free trial Quarks class and orientation at the Quantum Dojo. Photo by Stephanie Court Toscano. Reviews Facebook Community Dojo Quantum Martial Arts is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit in the State of California, and we thrive on the generosity of people like you. All donations are tax-deductible. Quantum’s tax ID number is 26-1534597. No donation is too small, or too large. Whether it’s $20 or $20,000, every cent will be hugely appreciated and put to good use. Support Our Kids Donate now to support our youth scholarship program which allows families in need to train at reduced cost. We believe investment in our community involves investing in our youth. Our Sister Schools Our Seattle Dojo has served thousands of students since opening on Capitol Hill in 1995, and currently offers youth and adult classes in the Central District. Our Portland Dojo was established in 2020, and currently offers youth and adult classes in Southeast Portland. A WordPress.com Website .
